conflict: The double bind of being both the architect of Mort's destruction and the stranger who promised to help — visiting his father's store in the body of the man whose signature is on the demolition order
consequence: Nate enters the hardware store and makes a promise to his father that may be impossible to keep. The deception is no longer just personal survival — it's now an active relationship built on a lie that Nate cannot sustain or abandon.
must_land_beats: The hardware store interior: sawdust, WD-40, the wall of specialty fasteners — sensory memory colliding with present experience
Buying the level: Nate holding an object that connects him to childhood
Mort's pride in the store — sixteen years, built from nothing, the specificity of his inventory knowledge
Mort describing the redevelopment: not angry, tired — the tone of a man who has accepted he cannot fight the machine
Nate's promise: 'I work in real estate. I could look into it.' — spoken before he can stop himself
Walking out with the level and the promise, both useless, both heavy
pressure_source: The double bind of being both the architect of Mort's destruction and the stranger who promised to help — visiting his father's store in the body of the man whose signature is on the demolition order
state_shift: Nate enters the hardware store and makes a promise to his father that may be impossible to keep. The deception is no longer just personal survival — it's now an active relationship built on a lie that Nate cannot sustain or abandon.
